reason: The servant, in this case Abimelech, displays qualities of humility as he initially seeks to gain the favor of the men of Shechem by presenting himself as their brother and appealing to their sense of unity and commonality. This act of humility is evident in his attempt to establish himself as a leader by asking whether it is better for all the sons of Jerubbaal to reign over the people or just one, indicating a level of deference and humility in his approach.
